column1: # environment: Windows desktop, Compose-first Android UI, Mapbox token deferred by user

column7: description
homepage still lacks a draggable bottom sheet and the map stack is still Baidu-specific
Homepage switched from a fixed bottom panel to a draggable bottom-sheet scaffold, preparing the app for the Mapbox migration and a more natural map-first interaction model.
Bottom-sheet iteration failed during Gradle configuration because BuildConfig fields were added for future Mapbox token support without enabling buildFeatures.buildConfig.
BuildConfig support enabled and the homepage now uses a draggable bottom-sheet scaffold, keeping the app structurally ready for Mapbox token wiring in the next pass.
Homepage bottom-sheet iteration still failed verification because MainComposeScreen retained the unresolved MyLocation vector icon reference.

Homepage now uses a draggable bottom-sheet scaffold and the project is structurally prepared for deferred Mapbox token wiring; this pass changes interaction structure rather than reducing the polish gap yet.
Introduced a map provider abstraction and stopped Activities from constructing Baidu MapView directly, reducing the coupling before the actual Mapbox swap.
Map provider abstraction pass failed verification because MainActivity still typed dashboardMapView as MapView after the direct import cleanup.
Map provider abstraction now compiles: Activities no longer construct Baidu MapView directly, which clears the first structural blocker before the actual Mapbox SDK swap.
Fixed the BuildConfig token wiring so deferred Mapbox token support is now structurally safe and no longer breaks Java compilation.
Extracted Baidu-specific route rendering and camera logic from MapActivity into HistoryMapController, further reducing page-layer coupling before the actual Mapbox provider swap.
Map detail page now compiles with its Baidu-specific route rendering isolated inside HistoryMapController, reducing one more page-level blocker before Mapbox replacement.
Mapbox Maps SDK and Compose extension were added to the project and the runtime access token is now exported as an Android string resource for the next provider-swap step.
Mapbox SDK integration failed verification because Baidu Map and Mapbox both package libc++_shared.so, causing mergeDebugNativeLibs to stop on duplicate native libraries.
Resolved the Baidu/Mapbox native library collision and confirmed the project can now compile with the Mapbox Maps SDK and Compose extension present in the build.
Introduced a homepage map port abstraction so MainActivity now depends on a map controller contract instead of a Baidu-specific implementation.
Homepage map controller now compiles behind a HomeMapPort interface, giving both the homepage and detail map flows a replaceable entry point before the Mapbox provider implementation step.
Compose page layers no longer depend on the Baidu MapView type directly; they now accept a generic Android View host, which narrows the SDK-specific surface further.
Restored build stability after the neutral coordinate/bitmap refactor by routing Baidu-only controllers through compatibility extensions while preserving the Mapbox-ready abstractions.
Homepage and history maps now render through Mapbox Compose, all Baidu SDK dependencies and initialization were removed, and :app:assembleDebug passes without any Baidu references remaining.
